method: Perfect player vs near-perfect player over N games per variant: skill% = 2×(perfect win rate − 50%) clamped to [0,100], luck% = 100 − skill%. Ties count as half a win. 100% win rate ⇒ 100% skill; coin-flip ⇒ 0% skill.
skillModel: near-perfect player: takes the highest-EV option unless two or more options are within 4 EV points of the best, in which case it picks uniformly at random among that set. Options = every keep sub-multiset plus every legal score-now category (only legal categories at 0 rolls left).
